actor: AMD CFO Jean Hu + Investor Relations + Chief Accounting Officer
job: draft an AI-accelerator-only revenue line as a 10-Q segment footnote that splits MI300 / MI325 / MI450 from EPYC server CPUs inside the Data Center segment.
outcome: 10-Q footnote disclosing AI-accelerator-only revenue with optional supplementary unit-economics breakout, walked through external auditor review and referenced on the next earnings call.
motivation: Lisa Su has now publicly named Data Center the primary growth engine on the Q1 print, but buyside cannot triangulate accelerator-only ASP from the bundled line; voluntary disclosure pre-empts a more invasive SEC cloud-segment-style regime.

task.who: AMD CFO office + IR + chief accounting officer + outside auditor (Ernst & Young)
task.what: draft AI-accelerator-only revenue footnote with single-line-item plus supplementary breakout
task.where: AMD 10-Q filing + Q2 2026 earnings call deck + investor-day materials
task.when: May to August 2026 runway into the Q2 10-Q
task.why: Data Center bundle hides accelerator ASP; buyside disclosure ask is now operative
task.how: mirror MSFT $37B AI run-rate footnote shape (single-line with optional breakout)

actor: SEC Division of Corporation Finance staff + Office of the Chief Accountant
job: publish a comment-letter pattern or staff accounting bulletin signaling that bundled Data Center segment reporting is no longer sufficient when the issuer has publicly named AI accelerators the primary growth driver.
outcome: SEC staff comment letter (or Dear-Issuer-style guidance) directed at AMD / NVIDIA / Broadcom requesting AI-accelerator revenue disaggregation under ASC 280 segment-reporting principles.
motivation: MSFT $37B AI run-rate single-line precedent + the May 4 Anthropic + OpenAI structured-equity JV launches harden the disclosure bar; staff cannot leave the Big-3 accelerator vendors below the bar set by a hyperscaler customer.

task.who: SEC Corp Fin disclosure-review branch + OCA segment-reporting specialists
task.what: issue ASC 280 segment-disaggregation comment letter on AI-accelerator revenue
task.where: SEC EDGAR comment-letter file + staff accounting bulletin + speeches at AICPA conference
task.when: Q2 2026 review cycles through Q3 2026 filings
task.why: issuer-named primary growth driver must be separately reportable under ASC 280
task.how: ASC 280 chief-operating-decision-maker review + MSFT precedent referenced as comparable

actor: Big-3 hyperscaler AI-infrastructure procurement leads (Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google Cloud) plus their sell-side semiconductor analyst counterparties (Bernstein, Morgan Stanley, Mizuho, RBC, DA Davidson)
job: drive the buyside disclosure ask by writing AI-accelerator-only revenue segmentation into procurement scorecards and into client-facing valuation models so vendors that opt out of footnote disclosure face an explicit valuation discount.
outcome: Hyperscaler vendor-scorecards plus published sell-side coverage notes that treat AI-accelerator-revenue segmentation as the disclosure baseline through the H2 2026 earnings cycles.
motivation: $700B 2026 Big-Tech AI-capex tape forces buyside discipline; without an accelerator-only line the customer cannot model vendor unit economics or roadmap risk against MI450 + Helios competitive engagement.

task.who: hyperscaler chip-procurement leads + corporate FP&A + sell-side semis equity research desks
task.what: publish coverage notes and procurement scorecards demanding AI-accelerator footnote segmentation
task.where: client research notes + Bloomberg dashboards + earnings-preview decks + RFP scorecards
task.when: May to October 2026 covering Q2 and Q3 2026 earnings cycles
task.why: bundled Data Center line blocks unit-economics modelling on $700B AI-capex tape
task.how: compare AMD disclosure to MSFT $37B AI run-rate precedent; price-in opt-out discount
